vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Provide structural support for the wing skin and spar.
Integrate control surfaces with hinges.
Facilitate thermal management and service access.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on motor mount due to cooling inlet proximity
risk.mit: Increase distance between motor mount and cooling inlet

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity at wing spar reinforcement flange
risk.mit: Reinforce flange area with additional material or design changes

risk.desc: Possibility of misalignment during assembly due to complex interfaces
risk.mit: Implement more precise alignment aids and training

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate cooling airflow due to design constraints
risk.mit: Optimize cooling inlet design for better airflow

risk.desc: Potential difficulty in accessing service panels for maintenance
risk.mit: Improve service panel design for easier access

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structures: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
Evidence features: spar_socket, main_spar, anti_rotation_key, rib, carry_through, frame
Datum visibility: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Clearance named: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Interface count: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Standard fit vocabulary: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Standard hardware dimensioning: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes
Spar datum access: Spar socket, anti-rotation keys, and center datum remain visible and inspectable
Mission payload non-weapon boundary: Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
CG battery service: Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Load path continuity: Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer

part_tree/geometry_ops
interface_count
joint_evidence_rule
required_evidence_features

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex snap-fit interfaces
risk.mit: Implement visual alignment aids and clear assembly instructions

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting structural integrity over long flights
risk.mit: Use thermally stable materials and incorporate expansion joints

risk.desc: Battery bay may not provide sufficient clearance for all battery types
risk.mit: Specify battery type and ensure bay dimensions accommodate it

risk.desc: Sensor pocket might interfere with wing flexure if not properly designed
risk.mit: Review sensor placement and adjust pocket dimensions accordingly

risk.desc: Service access cut may weaken spar structure if too large
risk.mit: Optimize cut size to balance accessibility and structural integrity

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.path: Lift force from airfoil to fuselage via spar, ribs, and carry-through
Structural continuity from wingtip to wingtip, ensuring uniform load distribution
Heat dissipation from composite materials to ambient air

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate with wing skin for aerodynamic performance
Support mission-specific sensor or camera modules
Provide easy access for battery replacement and maintenance

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for fit and finish

verification_focus.description: Ensure the spar does not exceed 250g
Verify spar can withstand 800N of lift force

verify.rule: All required fields are present and correctly formatted.
The estimated mass does not exceed the allocated mass budget.
Each P0 internal_feature_target appears as a part_tree child and geometry_ops id/target.
Adjacent-module interfaces and mounting datums are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to press-fit tolerance.
risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and use alignment pins.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing stress on bolted connections.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion compensating materials or design for expansion.

risk.desc: Possibility of insufficient clearance leading to jamming of revolute bearing.
risk.mit: Increase clearance or use self-lubricating bearings.

risk.desc: Risk of uneven distribution of load due to improper placement of lightening pockets.
risk.mit: Optimize the placement and size of lightening pockets through finite element analysis.

risk.desc: Potential for misalignment of spar socket due to vertical support orientation.
risk.mit: Adjust support orientation or add alignment features.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Structural Support and Control Surface Integration

physics_paths.description: Transfers aerodynamic lift and structural loads from the wing skin to the wing spar and fuselage.
Manages heat dissipation from internal components to the exterior skin.
Provides access for maintenance and inspection of internal components.

integration_opportunities.description: Integration point for wing-mounted sensors or cameras.
Routing path for wiring or fluid lines.
Smooth transition between wing ribs and skin.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the subsystem does not exceed the allocated mass budget.
Verify that the load path is continuous from the wing skin to the fuselage.
Check that all mechanical interfaces fit within specified clearances.
